flashcards.definition: Physical breakdown (chewing, churning, segmentation) increasing surface area for enzymes.
Enzymatic breakdown of macromolecules into absorbable units (monosaccharides, amino acids, fatty acids).
Enzyme in saliva that begins starch digestion in the mouth.
Protein-digesting enzyme active in the stomach's acidic environment (pH ~2).
Produced by liver, stored in gallbladder; emulsifies fats for lipase action.
